title: Analysis context brief: interpret refuted real-hardware experiment
summary: The parent experiment produced a refuted result because the improved predictor missed the ≥75% NCU bottleneck-accuracy gate while passing MAPE and within-30% gates. This analysis determines whether the residual bottleneck gap warrants another experiment or is small enough to accept with documented limitations.

sections.evidence_question: Does the improved predictor meet all usefulness thresholds, and what is the smallest follow-up that can close the remaining bottleneck-accuracy gap?
sections.comparison_target: MAPE ≤25%, ≥75% of predictions within 30%, and ≥75% NCU bottleneck accuracy.
sections.stop_condition: Stop once the error sources are identified and a route decision (experiment or finalize) is recorded.
